steps.title: Identify the annual decay factor
steps.content: In $f(x) = 6,480(0.34)^{\frac{x}{12}}$, the exponent $\frac{x}{12}$ converts months to years. The base 0.34 is the year-over-year multiplication factor.

steps.title: Apply the decay formula to solve for p
steps.content:
A yearly decrease of $p\%$ means the value is multiplied by $(1 - \frac{p}{100})$ each year. Setting this equal to the decay factor:
$$1 - \frac{p}{100} = 0.34$$
$$\frac{p}{100} = 0.66$$
$$p = 66$$
That matches Choice D — 66.
